rationale: Reduce Windows CLI argument-length risk by standardizing LLM invocation to prefer payload files over long inline args, without touching code.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.llm_invocation_defaults.v1",
  "prefer_payload_file": true,
  "max_inline_chars": 8000,
  "arg_pack_mode": "file",
  "notes": "Use temp payload files to avoid Windows CLI arg-length limits; keep inline args short."
}

risks: Downstream code may ignore or not yet consume this procedural defaults file; no behavior change until wired.
expected_benefit: Provides a centralized, low-risk default to minimize inline CLI args, reducing failure risk on Windows.
